· New CD entry

A Album details

B Purchase

Landing cost—
Total cost—
Expected sales rate—

Expected sales rate = total cost + margin %, rounded to the nearest ₹10. Set the margin under Master Lists → Pricing.

C Ripping & condition

—

In-stock status is calculated automatically: a posted date in the Sale section below marks it Sold; a payment received date with no posted date marks it Booked; otherwise it's In Stock. Use the override above only for CDs that can't be sold (damaged, lost, gifted, etc).

D Sale

Profit / loss—
Status preview—

· Master lists

These are the option lists behind every dropdown in the Add / Edit form — Album details, Purchase, Ripping & condition, and Sale. Add a value here and it's selectable everywhere immediately; delete one to retire it going forward (records that already used it keep their saved value regardless).

· Pricing

Margin for expected sales rate

Every CD's Expected sales rate (shown on the Add / Edit form under Purchase) is calculated as total cost + this margin %, rounded to the nearest ₹10. Change it here and it applies to every CD immediately.

%

· Dropdown option lists

—

· Vendors & Customers

Pre-register a vendor or customer name here so it shows up in the Payment/Receipt Party dropdown right away — otherwise a name only appears there once you've actually used it on a purchase or sale.

Vendors

Customers

· Sub-Groups

Define sub-groups once with their main group locked in — e.g. "Household Expense" → Expense — so picking a sub-group on a Ledger below auto-fills the correct Group and you can't accidentally file an expense sub-group under Income (or vice versa).

· Ledgers (Chart of Accounts)

Define every ledger you want to track — e.g. "Rent", "Electricity", "EMI - Car Loan", "Freelance Income" — and tag each one as Asset, Liability, Expense, or Income. The Category dropdowns in the Budget and Day-to-Day tabs are built from the Expense/Income ledgers defined here.

· Bank & cash balance

· Data & backup

How saving works

Everything lives in a real SQLite database running inside this page (via sql.js / WebAssembly) — no server, no internet connection required. After every change, the database is auto-saved into this browser's local storage (IndexedDB) for this file. That storage is tied to your browser + device, so it's smart to also export a backup file regularly, especially before clearing browser data or moving to a new computer.

Backup file (.sqlite)

Download a complete snapshot, or load one back in (this replaces everything currently in the tracker).

Export

Export the full inventory as CSV for Excel / Google Sheets.

Danger zone

Wipes the current database in this browser and reloads the original starter data — CDs, HiFi & Other Equipment, Blank CDs, Cassettes, Customs, and the Vendor Ledger — from your uploaded spreadsheets.

Groove Ledger — runs entirely in your browser · SQLite via sql.js · no data leaves this device
